rationale: Both candidates independently found the strongest real bug in the diff — the varlen_func-may-be-None crash in the masked/piecewise path — matching RuixiangMa's GT comment almost exactly, and both flag the FA2/FA3 impl duplication. Neither touches the several doc/test-marker nits (kernels version requirements, skipif/hardware marks) that make up half the GT thread, so recall is low and roughly tied. Y is more precise: its 'unguarded final get_kernel() call crashes' finding is directly verifiable from the shown diff (the third get_kernel call truly has no enclosing try/except), and its validated-sweep notes show it also checked and correctly ruled out a false alarm (SM90 gating). X's headline finding #1 (integer version= silently no-ops) is speculative and is essentially contradicted by the author's own GT reply that version=1/2 are legitimate git-branch tags, undercutting precision despite X's own hedge.
